subject: Geography
title: The Atlas Mountains
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Atlas Mountains usable.

hook.text: The Atlas Mountains block the Sahara from swallowing Morocco and Algeria.

buildup.text: Named after the Greek titan who held up the sky, they stretch 2,500 km across North Africa.

discovery.text: Their northern slopes catch Mediterranean rain, keeping the coast green and fertile.

twist.text: South of the Atlas, rainfall drops to almost zero. The desert begins immediately.

climax.text: Berber communities have lived in these mountains for thousands of years, adapting to extremes.

punchline.text: One mountain range holds back an entire desert.

quiz.question: What do the Atlas Mountains block from spreading north?
